title: The Haringkraam Ritual
description: Learn how to order the famous Dutch herring and handle payments at a fish stand.

content.text: Welcome! In this lesson, you will learn how to order 'haring' (herring), a famous Dutch snack, from a fish stand. You will also learn numbers and how to pay.

data.phrase: Ik wil graag een broodje haring.
data.meaning: I would like a herring sandwich.

content.title: Quick Practice: Order Herring
content.expected_concept: The user should respond about onions using 'Ja, graag' or 'Nee, bedankt' or 'Met uitjes' or 'Zonder uitjes'

content.title: Ordering Herring
content.setting: You arrive at a bustling fish stand ('haringkraam'). Time to order!

conversation_flow.title: Greet and order a herring sandwich
Say yes or no to onions
Say yes or no to pickles

content.title: Numbers 1 to 10
content.explanation:
Dutch numbers 1 to 10:

1 = een
2 = twee
3 = drie
4 = vier
5 = vijf
6 = zes
7 = zeven
8 = acht
9 = negen
10 = tien

Note: 'een' means both 'a/an' and 'one'. You will need these for prices!

data.meaning: That is five euros. (for stating prices)
Then I will pay with cash.

content.title: Asking 'Can I...?' with 'Kan ik...?'
content.explanation:
To ask if you can do something, use 'Kan ik...?' followed by what you want to do.

Kan ik pinnen? = Can I pay by card?

This is a very useful pattern for making polite requests in Dutch!

content.title: Quick Practice: Ask the Price
content.expected_concept: The user should ask about the price using 'Hoeveel is het?' or ask to pay by card using 'Kan ik pinnen?'

content.title: The Payment Problem
content.setting: Your herring is ready. Time to pay, but there might be a problem...

conversation_flow.title: Ask how much it costs using 'Hoeveel is het?'
conversation_flow.chatbot_message: Alstublieft, een broodje haring met uitjes!

conversation_flow.title: Ask to pay by card using 'Kan ik pinnen?'
Say you will pay cash using 'Dan betaal ik contant.'

content.text: Goed gedaan! You can now order herring, customize your order, and handle payments in Dutch. In the next lesson, we will visit a Dutch café!
